text: ŠĻ¨√Ę-H√Ę .
We have not revealed the Quran to you ňĻO Prophetňļ to cause you distress,
but as a reminder to those in awe ňĻof Allahňļ.
ňĻIt isňļ a revelation from the One Who created the earth and the high heavens‚ÄĒ
the Most Compassionate, ňĻWho isňļ established on the Throne.
To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth and whatever is in between and whatever is underground.
Whether you speak openly ňĻor notňļ, He certainly knows what is secret and what is even more hidden.
Allah‚ÄĒthere is no god ňĻworthy of worshipňļ except Him. He has the Most Beautiful Names.
Has the story of Moses reached you ňĻO Prophetňļ?
When he saw a fire, he said to his family, ‚ÄúWait here, ňĻforňļ I have spotted a fire. Perhaps I can bring you a torch from it, or find some guidance at the fire.‚ÄĚ <sup foot_note=587>587</sup>

foot_notes.587: Moses and his family lost their way in the dark while they were travelling from Midian to Egypt.

text: But when he approached it, he was called, ‚ÄúO Moses!
It is truly I. I am your Lord! So take off your sandals, for you are in the sacred valley of ŠĻ¨uwa.
I have chosen you, so listen to what is revealed:
‚ÄėIt is truly I. I am Allah! There is no god ňĻworthy of worshipňļ except Me. So worship Me ňĻaloneňļ, and establish prayer for My remembrance.
The Hour is sure to come. My Will is to keep it hidden, so that every soul may be rewarded according to their efforts.
So do not let those who disbelieve in it and follow their desires distract you from it, or you will be doomed.‚Äô‚ÄĚ
ňĻAllah added,ňļ ‚ÄúAnd what is that in your right hand, O Moses?‚ÄĚ
He replied, ‚ÄúIt is my staff! I lean on it, and with it I beat down ňĻbranchesňļ for my sheep, and have other uses for it.‚ÄĚ <sup foot_note=588>588</sup>

foot_notes.588: Moses could have just said, ‚ÄúA staff.‚ÄĚ But he volunteered to talk about the uses of the staff, adding that there are many other uses he has not mentioned, with the hope that Allah would ask him about it more. The reason is that Moses wanted to prolong the conversation with Allah as much as he could.

text: Allah said, ‚ÄúThrow it down, O Moses!‚ÄĚ
So he did, then‚ÄĒbehold!‚ÄĒit became a serpent, slithering.
Allah said, ‚ÄúTake it, and have no fear. We will return it to its former state.
And put your hand under your armpit, it will come out ňĻshiningňļ white, unblemished, <sup foot_note=589>589</sup> as another sign,

foot_notes.589: Moses, who was dark-skinned, was asked to put his hand under his armpit. When he took it out it was shining white, but not out of a skin condition like melanoma.

text: so that We may show you some of Our greatest signs.
Go to Pharaoh, for he has truly transgressed ňĻall boundsňļ.‚ÄĚ
Moses prayed, ‚ÄúMy Lord! Uplift my heart for me,
and make my task easy,
and remove the impediment from my tongue
so people may understand my speech, <sup foot_note=590>590</sup>

foot_notes.590: Moses ( Ô∑ļ ) put a brand of fire in his mouth, which hindered his speech as he grew up. In this verse, he prays to Allah to help him speak clearly, and his prayer is answered.

text: and grant me a helper from my family,
Aaron, my brother.
Strengthen me through him,
and let him share my task,
so that we may glorify You much
and remember You much,
for truly You have ňĻalwaysňļ been overseeing us.‚ÄĚ
Allah responded, ‚ÄúAll that you requested has been granted, O Moses!
‚ÄúAnd surely We had shown You favour before,
when We inspired your mother with this:

‚ÄėPut him into a chest, then put it into the river. The river will wash it ashore, and he will be taken by ňĻPharaoh,ňļ an enemy of Mine and his.‚Äô And I blessed you with lovability from Me <sup foot_note=591>591</sup> ňĻO Mosesňļ so that you would be brought up under My ňĻwatchfulňļ Eye.

foot_notes.591: In other words, Allah made Moses a likeable person so Pharaoh and his wife would agree to keep him.

text: ňĻRememberňļ when your sister came along and proposed, ‚ÄėShall I direct you to someone who will nurse him?‚Äô <sup foot_note=592>592</sup> So We reunited you with your mother so that her heart would be put at ease, and she would not grieve. ňĻLaterňļ you killed a man ňĻby mistakeňļ, but We saved you from sorrow, as well as other tests We put you through. Then you stayed for a number of years among the people of Midian. Then you came here as pre-destined, O Moses!

foot_notes.592: Moses had refused all wet-nurses that were brought for him.

text: And I have selected you for My service.
‚ÄúGo forth, you and your brother, with My signs and never falter in remembering Me.
Go, both of you, to Pharaoh, for he has truly transgressed ňĻall boundsňļ.
Speak to him gently, so perhaps he may be mindful ňĻof Meňļ or fearful ňĻof My punishmentňļ.‚ÄĚ
They both pleaded, ‚ÄúOur Lord! We fear that he may be quick to harm us or act tyrannically.‚ÄĚ
Allah reassured ňĻthemňļ, ‚ÄúHave no fear! I am with you, hearing and seeing.

So go to him and say, ‚ÄėIndeed we are both messengers from your Lord, so let the Children of Israel go with us, and do not oppress them. We have come to you with a sign from your Lord. And salvation will be for whoever follows the ňĻrightňļ guidance.
It has indeed been revealed to us that the punishment will be upon whoever denies ňĻthe truthňļ and turns away.‚Äô‚ÄĚ
Pharaoh asked, ‚ÄúWho then is the Lord of you two, O Moses?‚ÄĚ
He answered, ‚ÄúOur Lord is the One Who has given everything its ňĻdistinctiveňļ form, then guided ňĻitňļ.‚ÄĚ
Pharaoh asked, ‚ÄúAnd what about previous peoples?‚ÄĚ
He replied, ‚ÄúThat knowledge is with my Lord in a Record. My Lord neither falters nor forgets ňĻanythingňļ.‚ÄĚ
ňĻHe is the Oneňļ Who has laid out the earth for ňĻall ofňļ you, and set in it pathways for you, and sends down rain from the sky, causing various types of plants to grow,
ňĻsoňļ eat and graze your cattle. Surely in this are signs for people of sound judgment.
From the earth We created you, and into it We will return you, and from it We will bring you back again.
And We certainly showed Pharaoh all of Our signs, <sup foot_note=593>593</sup> but he denied them and refused ňĻto believeňļ.
He said, ‚ÄúHave you come to drive us out of our land with your magic, O Moses?
We can surely meet you with similar magic. So set for us an appointment that neither of us will fail to keep, in a central place.‚ÄĚ
Moses said, ‚ÄúYour appointment is on the Day of the Festival, and let the people be gathered mid-morning.‚ÄĚ
Pharaoh then withdrew, orchestrated his scheme, then returned.
Moses warned the magicians, ‚ÄúWoe to you! Do not fabricate a lie against Allah, <sup foot_note=594>594</sup> or He will wipe you out with a torment. Whoever fabricates ňĻliesňļ is bound to fail.‚ÄĚ

foot_notes.594: By saying that Moses‚Äô miracle is pure magic.

text: So the magicians disputed the matter among themselves, conversing privately.
They concluded, ‚ÄúThese two are only magicians who want to drive you out of your land with their magic, and do away with your most cherished traditions.
So orchestrate your plan, then come forward in ňĻperfectňļ ranks. And whoever prevails today will certainly be successful.‚ÄĚ
They said, ‚ÄúO Moses! Either you cast, or let us be the first to cast.‚ÄĚ
Moses responded, ‚ÄúNo, you go first.‚ÄĚ And suddenly their ropes and staffs appeared to him‚ÄĒby their magic‚ÄĒto be slithering.
So Moses concealed fear within himself.
We reassured ňĻhimňļ, ‚ÄúDo not fear! It is certainly you who will prevail.
Cast what is in your right hand, and it will swallow up what they have made, for what they have made is no more than a magic trick. And magicians can never succeed wherever they go.‚ÄĚ
So the magicians fell down in prostration, declaring, ‚ÄúWe believe in the Lord of Aaron and Moses.‚ÄĚ

Pharaoh threatened, ‚ÄúHow dare you believe in him before I give you permission? He must be your master who taught you magic. I will certainly cut off your hands and feet on opposite sides, and crucify you on the trunks of palm trees. You will really see whose punishment is more severe and more lasting.‚ÄĚ

They responded, ‚ÄúBy the One Who created us! We will never prefer you over the clear proofs that have come to us. So do whatever you want! Your authority only covers the ňĻfleetingňļ life of this world.
Indeed, we have believed in our Lord so He may forgive our sins and that magic you have forced us to practice. And Allah is far superior ňĻin rewardňļ and more lasting ňĻin punishmentňļ.‚ÄĚ
Whoever comes to their Lord as an evildoer will certainly have Hell, where they can neither live nor die.
But whoever comes to Him as a believer, having done good, they will have the highest ranks:
the Gardens of Eternity, under which rivers flow, where they will stay forever. That is the reward of those who purify themselves.

And We surely inspired Moses, ňĻsaying,ňļ ‚ÄúLeave with My servants ňĻat nightňļ and strike a dry passage for them across the sea. Have no fear of being overtaken, nor be concerned ňĻof drowningňļ.‚ÄĚ
Then Pharaoh pursued them with his soldiers‚ÄĒbut how overwhelming were the waters that submerged them!
And ňĻsoňļ Pharaoh led his people astray, and did not guide ňĻthem rightlyňļ.

O Children of Israel! We saved you from your enemy, and made an appointment with you <sup foot_note=595>595</sup> on the right side of Mount ŠĻ¨√Ľr, and sent down to you manna and quails, <sup foot_note=596>596</sup>

foot_notes.595: i.e., your prophet to receive the Tablets for your guidance.
foot_notes.596: Manna (heavenly bread) and quails (chicken-like birds) sustained the children of Israel in the wilderness after they left Egypt.

text: ňĻsaying,ňļ ‚ÄúEat from the good things We have provided for you, but do not transgress in them, or My wrath will befall you. And whoever My wrath befalls is certainly doomed.
But I am truly Most Forgiving to whoever repents, believes, and does good, then persists on ňĻtrueňļ guidance.‚ÄĚ
ňĻAllah asked,ňļ ‚ÄúWhy have you come with such haste ahead of your people, O Moses?‚ÄĚ <sup foot_note=597>597</sup>

foot_notes.597: Moses selected a delegation of seventy people from his community to go to Mount ŠĻ¨√Ľr where it was appointed for him to receive the Tablets. On the way, he rushed for the appointment with Allah, arriving before the delegation.

text: He replied, ‚ÄúThey are close on my tracks. And I have hastened to You, my Lord, so You will be pleased.‚ÄĚ
Allah responded, ‚ÄúWe have indeed tested your people in your absence, and the S√Ęmiri <sup foot_note=598>598</sup> has led them astray.‚ÄĚ

foot_notes.598: The S√Ęmiri, or the man from Samaria, was a hypocrite who led the Children of Israel into idol-worship.

text: So Moses returned to his people, furious and sorrowful. He said, ‚ÄúO my people! Had your Lord not made you a good promise? <sup foot_note=599>599</sup> Has my absence been too long for you? Or have you wished for wrath from your Lord to befall you, so you broke your promise to me?‚ÄĚ <sup foot_note=600>600</sup>

foot_notes.599: To reveal the Torah for their guidance.
foot_notes.600: To worship Allah alone until Moses returned with the Tablets.

text: They argued, ‚ÄúWe did not break our promise to you of our own free will, but we were made to carry the burden of the people‚Äôs ňĻgoldenňļ jewellery, <sup foot_note=601>601</sup> then we threw it ňĻinto the fireňļ, and so did the S√Ęmiri.‚ÄĚ

foot_notes.601: The jewellery they borrowed from their Egyptian neighbours before they fled Egypt.

text: Then he moulded for them an idol of a calf that made a lowing sound. They said, ‚ÄúThis is your god and the god of Moses, but Moses forgot ňĻwhere it wasňļ!‚ÄĚ
Did they not see that it did not respond to them, nor could it protect or benefit them?
Aaron had already warned them beforehand, ‚ÄúO my people! You are only being tested by this, for indeed your ňĻone trueňļ Lord is the Most Compassionate. So follow me and obey my orders.‚ÄĚ
They replied, ‚ÄúWe will not cease to worship it until Moses returns to us.‚ÄĚ
Moses scolded ňĻhis brotherňļ, ‚ÄúO Aaron! What prevented you, when you saw them going astray,
from following after me? How could you disobey my orders?‚ÄĚ <sup foot_note=602>602</sup>

Aaron pleaded, ‚ÄúO son of my mother! Do not seize me by my beard or ňĻthe hair ofňļ my head. I really feared that you would say, ‚ÄėYou have caused division among the Children of Israel, and did not observe my word.‚Äô‚ÄĚ
Moses then asked, ‚ÄúWhat did you think you were doing, O S√Ęmiri?‚ÄĚ

He said, ‚ÄúI saw what they did not see, so I took a handful ňĻof dustňļ from the hoof-prints of ňĻthe horse ofňļ the messenger-angel ňĻGabrielňļ then cast it ňĻon the moulded calfňļ. This is what my lower-self tempted me into.‚ÄĚ <sup foot_note=603>603</sup>

foot_notes.603: This verse could also be translated as follows: ‚ÄúI had an insight which they did not have, then grasped some knowledge from the messenger ňĻMosesňļ, but ňĻlaterňļ threw it away. This is what my lower-self tempted me to do.‚ÄĚ According to many Quran commentators, while Moses and the Children of Israel were crossing the sea to escape abuse by Pharaoh and his people, the S√Ęmiri saw Gabriel on a horse leading the way, and every time the horse touched the ground, it turned green. So the S√Ęmiri took a handful of dust from the hoof-prints of the horse, and later tossed it at the calf so it started to make a lowing sound.

text: Moses said, ‚ÄúGo away then! And for ňĻthe rest of yourňļ life you will surely be crying, ‚ÄėDo not touch ňĻmeňļ!‚Äô <sup foot_note=604>604</sup> Then you will certainly have a fate <sup foot_note=605>605</sup> that you cannot escape. Now look at your god to which you have been devoted: we will burn it up, then scatter it in the sea completely.‚ÄĚ

foot_notes.604: Meaning, alienated in the dessert, away from the people.

text: ňĻThen Moses addressed his people,ňļ ‚ÄúYour only god is Allah, there is no god ňĻworthy of worshipňļ except Him. He encompasses everything in ňĻHisňļ knowledge.‚ÄĚ
This is how We relate to you ňĻO Prophetňļ some of the stories of the past. And We have certainly granted you a Reminder <sup foot_note=606>606</sup> from Us.
Whoever turns away from it will surely bear the burden ňĻof sinňļ on the Day of Judgment,
suffering its consequences forever. What an evil burden they will carry on Judgment Day!
ňĻBeware ofňļ the Day the Trumpet will be blown, <sup foot_note=607>607</sup> and We will gather the wicked on that Day blue-faced ňĻfrom horror and thirstňļ. <sup foot_note=608>608</sup>
They will whisper among themselves, ‚ÄúYou stayed no more than ten days ňĻon the earthňļ.‚ÄĚ
We know best what they will say‚ÄĒthe most reasonable of them will say, ‚ÄúYou stayed no more than a day.‚ÄĚ
And ňĻifňļ they ask you ňĻO Prophetňļ about the mountains, ňĻthenňļ say, ‚ÄúMy Lord will wipe them out completely,
leaving the earth level and bare,
with neither depressions nor elevations to be seen.‚ÄĚ

On that Day all will follow the caller ňĻfor assemblyňļ, ňĻandňļ none will dare to deviate. All voices will be hushed before the Most Compassionate. Only whispers <sup foot_note=609>609</sup> will be heard.
On that Day no intercession will be of any benefit, except by those granted permission by the Most Compassionate and whose words are agreeable to Him.
He ňĻfullyňļ knows what is ahead of them and what is behind them, <sup foot_note=610>610</sup> but they cannot encompass Him in ňĻtheirňļ knowledge. <sup foot_note=611>611</sup>

foot_notes.610: Allah fully knows what fate awaits them and what they did in the world.
foot_notes.611: Or ‚Äú‚Ä¶ they cannot encompass His knowledge.‚ÄĚ

text: And all faces will be humbled before the Ever-Living, All-Sustaining. And those burdened with wrongdoing will be in loss.
But whoever does good and is a believer will have no fear of being wronged or denied ňĻtheir rewardňļ.
And so We have sent it down as an Arabic Quran and varied the warnings in it, so perhaps they will shun evil or it may cause them to be mindful.

Exalted is Allah, the True King! Do not rush to recite ňĻa revelation ofňļ the Quran ňĻO Prophetňļ before it is ňĻproperlyňļ conveyed to you, <sup foot_note=612>612</sup> and pray, ‚ÄúMy Lord! Increase me in knowledge.‚ÄĚ

foot_notes.612: The Prophet ( Ô∑ļ ) was eager to recite the Quran while it was being revealed to him through the angel Gabriel. So he ( Ô∑ļ ) was told to take his time to learn it by heart once the verses are properly delivered to him.

text: And indeed, We once made a covenant with Adam, but he forgot, and ňĻsoňļ We did not find determination in him.
And ňĻrememberňļ when We said to the angels, ‚ÄúProstrate before Adam,‚ÄĚ so they all did‚ÄĒbut not Ibl√ģs , <sup foot_note=613>613</sup> who refused ňĻarrogantlyňļ.
So We cautioned, ‚ÄúO Adam! This is surely an enemy to you and to your wife. So do not let him drive you both out of Paradise, for you ňĻO Adamňļ would then suffer ňĻhardshipňļ.
Here it is guaranteed that you will never go hungry or unclothed,
nor will you ňĻeverňļ suffer from thirst or ňĻthe sun‚Äôsňļ heat.‚ÄĚ <sup foot_note=614>614</sup>

foot_notes.614: There is no sun or cold weather in Paradise, only light. See 76:13.

text: But Satan whispered to him, saying, ‚ÄúO Adam! Shall I show you the Tree of Immortality and a kingdom that does not fade away?‚ÄĚ

So they both ate from the tree and then their nakedness was exposed to them, prompting them to cover themselves with leaves from Paradise. So Adam disobeyed his Lord, and ňĻsoňļ lost his way. <sup foot_note=615>615</sup>

foot_notes.615: Unlike the Bible (Genesis 3), the Quran does not blame Eve for Adam‚Äôs fall from the Garden.

text: Then his Lord chose him ňĻfor His graceňļ, accepted his repentance, and guided him ňĻrightlyňļ.

Allah said, ‚ÄúDescend, both of you, from here together ňĻwith Satanňļ as enemies to each other. Then when guidance comes to you from Me, whoever follows My guidance will neither go astray ňĻin this lifeňļ nor suffer ňĻin the nextňļ.
But whoever turns away from My Reminder will certainly have a miserable life, <sup foot_note=616>616</sup> then We will raise them up blind on the Day of Judgment.‚ÄĚ

foot_notes.616: The Reminder is another name for the Quran.

text: They will cry, ‚ÄúMy Lord! Why have you raised me up blind, although I used to see?‚ÄĚ
Allah will respond, ‚ÄúIt is so, just as Our revelations came to you and you neglected them, so Today you are neglected.‚ÄĚ
This is how We reward whoever transgresses and does not believe in the revelations of their Lord. And the punishment of the Hereafter is far more severe and more lasting.
Is it not yet clear to them how many peoples We destroyed before them, whose ruins they still pass by? Surely in this are signs for people of sound judgment.
Had it not been for a prior decree from your Lord <sup foot_note=617>617</sup> ňĻO Prophetňļ and a term already set, their ňĻinstantňļ doom would have been inevitable.

foot_notes.617: That He will delay their judgment until the Hereafter.

text: So be patient ňĻO Prophetňļ with what they say. And glorify the praises of your Lord before sunrise and before sunset, and glorify Him in the hours of the night and at both ends of the day, <sup foot_note=618>618</sup> so that you may be pleased ňĻwith the rewardňļ.

foot_notes.618: This verse refers to the times of the five daily prayers.

text: Do not let your eyes crave what We have allowed some of the disbelievers to enjoy; the ňĻfleetingňļ splendour of this worldly life, which We test them with. But your Lord‚Äôs provision ňĻin the Hereafterňļ is far better and more lasting.
Bid your people to pray, and be diligent in ňĻobservingňļ it. We do not ask you to provide. It is We Who provide for you. And the ultimate outcome is ňĻonlyňļ for ňĻthe people ofňļ righteousness.

They demand, ‚ÄúIf only he could bring us a sign from his Lord!‚ÄĚ <sup foot_note=619>619</sup> Have they not ňĻalreadyňļ received a confirmation of what is in earlier Scriptures? <sup foot_note=620>620</sup>

foot_notes.619: The pagans of Mecca were not satisfied with a literary miracle in the form of the Quran, so they asked for a tangible miracle ( see 17:90-91) similar to the staff of Moses.
foot_notes.620: This either refers to the Biblical stories confirmed by the Quran or the prophecies of MuŠł•ammad ( Ô∑ļ ) in the Bible. Some Muslim scholars cite Deuteronomy 18:15-18 and 33:2, Isaiah 42, and John 14:16 as examples of the description of Prophet MuŠł•ammad ( Ô∑ļ ) in the Bible. However, Bible scholars interpret these verses differently.

text: Had We destroyed them with a torment before this ňĻProphet cameňļ, they would have surely argued, ‚ÄúOur Lord! If only You had sent us a messenger, we would have followed Your revelations before being humiliated and put to shame.‚ÄĚ
Say ňĻto them, O Prophetňļ, ‚ÄúEach ňĻof usňļ is waiting, so keep waiting! You will soon know who is on the Straight Path and is ňĻrightlyňļ guided.‚ÄĚ
